story: France secretly joined the fight against Britain! On July 23, 1777, King Louis XVI and his Foreign Minister made a sneaky plan. They agreed to send weapons to the American colonies, helping them in their struggle for independence. This secret support played a big role in the American Revolution's success.
activity_question: What did King Louis XVI of France agree to send to the American colonies?
follow_up_question: Why do you think France wanted to help the American colonies fight against Britain?

title: A Polish Hero Joins the American Revolution
story: Hooves clattered on cobblestones as Casimir Pulaski rode into Marblehead, Massachusetts on July 23, 1777. This brave Polish military leader had traveled across the ocean to help the American colonies fight for independence. Pulaski volunteered his skills as a cavalry expert, ready to train and lead soldiers on horseback in the Continental Army.
activity_question: What did Casimir Pulaski volunteer to do in the Continental Army?
follow_up_question: Why do you think cavalry (soldiers on horseback) was important in the American Revolution?

title: Tiny Gland, Big Discovery
story: Scientists at Yale University made a breakthrough in 1937. They isolated a hormone from the pituitary gland, a pea-sized powerhouse in our brains. This tiny gland controls important body functions. The discovery on July 23 helped doctors better understand how our bodies work and grow.
activity_question: What did scientists at Yale University isolate in 1937?
activity_choices: A hormone from the pituitary gland
A new type of brain cell
activity_answer: A hormone from the pituitary gland
follow_up_question: Why is the pituitary gland called a powerhouse?

title: Tinian Takeover: A Turning Point
story: Boom! The battle for Tinian began. In 1944, US forces stormed this tiny Pacific island on July 23. Why was this speck of land so important? Tinian became a crucial airbase, launching the planes that ended World War II. The invasion marked a key step towards victory in the Pacific.
activity_question: What did Tinian become after the US invasion?
follow_up_question: Why do you think having an airbase close to Japan was important for the US in World War II?

title: Jets Take Flight in the Navy
story: Zoom! The skies above Rhode Island buzzed with excitement in 1947. On July 23, the US Navy launched its very first squadron of jet aircraft at Quonset Point. These speedy planes marked a big leap forward for naval aviation, zooming faster than ever before.
activity_question: Where did the US Navy launch its first jet squadron?
follow_up_question: How do you think jet planes changed naval warfare?

title: China's Carbon Challenge
story: China faced a big environmental challenge in 2018. While making progress on new emission goals, the country's carbon dioxide output soared. That year, China released about 9.2 billion metric tons of this greenhouse gas. Surprisingly, this amount was more than the United States and Europe produced together on July 23.
activity_question: What greenhouse gas did China release in large amounts?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for countries to set emission targets?

title: Caravaggio's Big Break in Rome
story: Brushes ready! In 1599, a talented Italian painter named Caravaggio got his big chance. On July 23, he received his first public job to paint the Contarelli Chapel in Rome. This special task would show off his amazing skills in a famous church called San Luigi dei Francesi.
activity_question: What was the name of the chapel Caravaggio was hired to paint?
follow_up_question: Why do you think getting a public commission was important for an artist like Caravaggio?
